body:
Don't think this has been asked yet so here's an obvious one:

Favourite book or author?

And what are you reading now?
Let your kid enjoy whatever she wants to read, don’t be the woke equivalent of my parents who confiscated *The Golden Compass

>She says I have read 0 books, just listened to someone reading

I wouldn't throw that in the face of someone who can't read/read easily because of a disability, so I wouldn't say it to anyone.  What would be the point?  I mainly listen to audiobooks nowadays, for various reasons, and I describe myself as having listened to books,  but if someone said "read", I'd be fine with that.  And would have been back when I was a voracious reader, too.

Terry Pratchett's Discworld series has aged very well. So have a lot of Diana Wynne Jones' books (like Charmed Life, The Time of the Ghost, and Power of Three for example).

I also think that the Lyonesse trilogy by Jack Vance has aged quite well, unlike some of his other works.

And of course The Hobbit and The Lord of the Rings are now timeless classics.

And I think you're what most people would call insufferably smug. 

Although considering your post history I guess I shouldn't be surprised.

You are now making unfounded assumptions. I am not a republican, nor am I American or in favour of book banning, or lacking in a proper sexual education. They take those kinds of things very seriously in Canada. You are lacking not only in knowledge of children, what actually is age appropriate at what age, and what a proper education is, it seems your upbringing was also lacking since your parents clearly didn’t know what they were doing for you to turn out like this, and think of yourself as superior and everyone else failing in some way.
